Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels

  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

commit.message:
feat: production deployment, auth fixes, onboarding calendar step

Docker & Deployment:
- Multi-stage Dockerfile building shared/engine/api (no web)
- docker-compose with PostgreSQL, env var passthrough
- DEPLOY.md with full VPS + Cloudflare Pages instructions
- Trust proxy support (TRUST_PROXY env var) for rate limiting behind reverse proxy

Auth & Cookie Fixes:
- Switch sameSite from 'strict' to 'lax' for OAuth redirect compatibility
- Drop __Host- cookie prefix for cross-subdomain support
- Add COOKIE_DOMAIN env var for cross-subdomain cookies (.theflyingrat.com)
- Lazy OAuth states table creation (fixes ESM import hoisting race with dotenv)
- Switch frontend from $env/static/public to import.meta.env for monorepo compat

WebSocket Fixes:
- Fix permanent "You're offline" banner (subscribeConnectionState now triggers connect)
- Fix disconnect/reconnect flicker on page navigation (keep WS alive while layout mounted)
- Fix infinite progress bar (navigating.to instead of navigating object truthiness)

Onboarding:
- Add "Choose your calendars" step (step 3) with toggle/mode controls
- Auto-discover calendars after Google OAuth connection
- Hide back button when at minimum step after Google auth
- Clamp ?step= query param to valid range
- Move shared cal-table and spinning styles to _components.scss

Startup Validation:
- Add COOKIE_DOMAIN production warning
- ENCRYPTION_KEY format validation (64 hex chars)
- DATABASE_URL production requirement check

commit.message:
fix: peripheral detection broken by CC:T 1.20 multi-type getType()

In CC:Tweaked 1.20.1, peripheral.getType() returns multiple values
(e.g. "minecraft:chest", "inventory"). The scanner only captured the
first value, which is the block registry name — never matching our
handler keys like "inventory" or "me_bridge".

Fix: collect all returned types and use the first one present in the
allowed filter, so chests, ME bridges, and all other peripherals are
now correctly detected regardless of type ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Task 19: Env-hide (#21)

* Task 19: feat: add env-hide module with shouldHide and integration

Add client/src/env-hide.js exporting shouldHide(envHideList, environment)
that checks if the current environment matches any entry in the hide list
(case-insensitive). Integrate into main.js init flow: after fetch, check
response environment against config.envHide and skip rendering if matched.

Tests cover: matching/non-matching environments, case-insensitive matching,
multiple environments, null/empty/undefined inputs, and integration with
full init flow.

  is a plain string instead of an array (review comment 1)
- Add logger.log when banner is hidden by env-hide for diagnostics
  (review comment 2)
- Add logger.log when envHide is configured but server response lacks

- Update JSDoc to document missing-environment behavior

commit.message:
Initial release: local Claude Code efficiency dashboard

- Parser reads ~/.claude session JSONL files, extracts tokens, costs, tool calls, prompts
- Scorer computes per-session efficiency score (0-100) across 5 dimensions
- Tips engine detects waste patterns (high tool ratio, low cache hits, cost inflection, model mismatch)
- Badge system rewards sustained good habits (Surgical Prompter, Cache Whisperer, etc.)
- Dashboard with session table, daily usage chart, drill-down session view with conversation timeline
- Subscription savings calculator (user-selected plan vs API-equivalent cost)
- Privacy-safe team export (no prompt text leaves the machine)
- Zero runtime dependencies — Node.js stdlib only
- Live reload for development (SSE file watcher)

commit.message:
#620 Fix: LLM now uses Vision analysis for user-sent images instead of saying "no access"

Root cause: When users send images (via /chat/image or /chat/document), Vision API
analyzes them successfully, but the [ANALISIS DE IMAGEN] tag was ambiguous — the LLM
didn't understand it should USE the analysis to respond, so it said "no tengo acceso
directo al calendario".

Changes:
- api_routes.py: Reformulate [ANALISIS DE IMAGEN] message as explicit directive telling
  LLM to use the analysis content and NEVER claim it can't see the image
- instructions_shared.txt: Add rule #620 explaining how to handle [ANALISIS DE IMAGEN]
  content — use it as visual information, never deny access

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
add mTLS certificate generation script

Helper script to generate a self-signed CA with server and client
certificates for frp mTLS authentication. Supports reusing an existing
CA, generating multiple server/client certs, and configurable key size
and validity. Sets restrictive file permissions automatically.

refactor(engine): consolidate history management and remove aggregation buffers

- Introduced HistoryManager to encapsulate ring buffers and merging logic.
- Removed pendingStateChanges, pendingParameterChanges, and pendingLogs from Want struct.
- Standardized on direct state updates while delegating grouping logic to HistoryManager.
- Simplified execution loop and agent state handling by eliminating manual aggregation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(validate): split U-06 into event edge cases (U-06) and data source fallback (U-07)

U-06 previously conflated event edge cases with data source fallback,
causing the LLM to produce generic "cancelled/postponed" messages when
the real issue was a missing fallback data source.

- U-06: now only about event edge cases (cancelled, postponed, etc.)
- U-07: new check for data source redundancy — fails when only one
  source URL is specified with no consensus/fallback language
- Link "Single data source with no fallback" risk factor (+30) to U-07
  so the risk score is consistent with the validation checks

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: cournot-protocol
repository.description: Proof of Reasoning AI Oracle

commit.message:
harden server config for production

- Add auth.additionalScopes to validate token on every heartbeat and
  new connection, not just at login
- Add allowPorts to restrict which ports clients can bind (default
  10000-50000), preventing abuse of system/well-known ports
- Add transport.maxPoolCount to cap connection pool per proxy
- Auto-detect mounted TLS certs for server identity verification
- Auto-detect mounted CA cert to enable mTLS client authentication
- Default dashboard bind address to 127.0.0.1 instead of 0.0.0.0
- Auto-enable dashboard TLS when server certs are available
- Add opt-in Prometheus metrics and subdomain restriction support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Deduplicate Max Moves in move list; fix Max Guard logging

- displayedMoves now uses reduce+Set so multiple moves of the same type
  collapse into a single Max Move entry (e.g. two Fire moves → one Max Flare)
- Max Guard logging was broken: battleContext() was called with no args
  (module-level fn requires pokemon/hp/... params), causing a silent throw.
  Fixed by building ctx the same way the normal roll path does.
- Roll button label updated to 'Log Max Guard' for clarity.

Merge feat/apple-notes-rich-content: sync bidirectionnelle contenu riche

Ajout du support complet du contenu riche Apple Notes :

- Lecture SQLite batch (attachements + protobuf, 2 requetes)
- HTML→MD enrichi (tables, strikethrough, blockquotes, code, listes)
- MD→HTML via markdown-it-py (post-processing Apple Notes)
- Fusion checkboxes par matching contenu normalise
- Injection attachements par contexte protobuf
- Frontend rendu adaptatif (PDF/audio/video/image)

76 tests, validation donnees reelles OK.
